rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Renewable Energy in Rural Areas
rows.description:
Decentralised renewable energies are not only the most climate-friendly option for supplying rural areas in Nepal with energy, they are usually also the most cost-effective. However, the country has so far lacked a strong renewable energy market shaped by private-sector initiative and competition that offers good, cost-effective products and services.
Since 2019, Nepalese municipalities have been authorised to develop smaller renewable energy power plants themselves, to take over the municipal energy supply and receive support for this from the German Federal Government. The transition to local and regional governments promoting decentralised renewable energy is challenging the public sector and private sector renewable energy providers. The private sector lacks the expertise to develop a renewable energy market under these new conditions.

Local and regional governments are developing a renewable energy market and expanding the private sectors knowledge of public financing. More people in rural areas of Nepal with thus be supplied with energy.

Through training, advisory services and organisational development, the project supports the capacities of local and regional governments, the state agency for renewable energies and the Government of Nepal in planning and promoting renewable energy. At the same time, it is establishing a digital platform to disseminate knowledge on renewable energy in rural communities, provide further training, offer advisory services and share experience.
The project also supports the private sector in identifying and disseminating new business models and solutions for renewable energy. It is organising an ideas competition and implementing selected concepts as models. Here, it works with government representatives to raise awareness of the competition and foster public promotion of ideas. The private sector and public officials are also developing their expertise in public innovation promotion.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Sustainable Economic Development in Rural and Semi-Urban Areas - MSME Finance Phase II
rows.description: The objective of the FC-module is the sustainable provision and use of a target-group oriented loan portfolio for predominantly rural and semi-urban micro, small and medium-sized enterprises (MSMEs) for their business investments (working capital and fixed capital investments). By better tailoring the financing offer to the needs of rural and semi-urban MSMEs, the FC-module contributes to the overall goal of improving the entrepreneurial ecosystem for sustainable and socially balanced economic development in Nepal. In the context of the covid-crisis, the FC-module shall further provide access to short-term bridge loans in order to support MSMEs liquidity.The indirect target group are predominantly rural and semi-urban MSMEs (incl. cooperatives and single entrepreneurs), which will benefit from improved access to a needs-based loan offer thanks to the additional refinancing and the MSME-tailored products. The direct target group are two selected banks - the commercial bank Siddhartha Bank Limited (SBL) and the development bank Muktinath Bikas Bank Limited (MNBBL) - which provide financial services to MSMEs and whose capacities and activities in the area of rural and semi-urban MSME finance will be strengthened.The total cost of the FC-module is EUR 14.5 million, of which in total EUR 7.0 million will be financed by a FC-grant: EUR 6.0 million FC-grant will be provided in form of a long-term loan for the refinancing of the MSME loan portfolio of the two partner financial institutions (PFIs) and EUR 1.0 million FC-grant will be provided for an Accompanying Measure. EUR 7.5 million will be provided as an own contribution by the PFIs to leverage the FC-funds for the refinancing line. Under the Accompanying Measure, the capacities and know-how of the PFIs (incl. crisis management) as well as of the MSMEs (particularly financial literacy) shall be strengthened. Moreover, the central bank Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB) shall be supported in the set-up and administration of a revolving fund for MSME Finance.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Improvement of Mother and Child Care in Urban Areas - Paropakar Maternity and Womens Hospital Kathmandu
rows.description: The module aims to improve access to and increase utilization of quality medical care for pregnant women and newborns at the selected satellite hospitals in the Kathmandu Valley, while reducing the burden on and specializing the Paropakar Maternity and Womens Hospital (Paropakar) Kathmandu. Additionally, the module aims to strengthen gender equality and empower women in leadership, management, service delivery and user groups in the catchment area of the satellite hospitals. The recipient is the Government of the Federal Democratic Republic of Nepal. The project executing agency, in addition to the Nepalese Ministry of Health, is Paropakar in Kathmandu. The direct target group is the population living in the Kathmandu Valley, especially women and children. The project measures include the construction of a new satellite hospital in Tarakeshwor, in the northwest of the Kathmandu Valley. The location of the satellite hospital is in the immediate periphery of the Kathmandu Valley, in order to relieve Paropakar of mainly normal and low-risk births and to alleviate existing bottlenecks in mother-child care. The project will also finance necessary medical equipment and the digital connection and networking between Paropakar and the satellite hospital. Measures to promote energy efficiency will also be implemented. A complementary measure is planned to train the executing agencys managerial and executive staff (Paropakar and satellite hospitals), especially in the management, handling, operation and maintenance of financed measures and environmental and social standards.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Improvement of Mother and Child Care in Urban Areas - Paropakar Maternity and Womens Hospital Kathmandu
rows.description: The new construction of the satellite hospitals under the module Support of mother-child care in urban areas - Paropakar maternity and gynecological hospital Kathmandu is flanked by a complementary measure. The accompanying measure is aimed on the one hand at the health personnel of Paropakar and the satellite hospitals in Chandragiri and Tarekeshwor, and on the other hand at the population who will use health services in these satellite hospitals. The main focus is on capacity development of the satellite hospitals to improve the quality of clinical care with a focus on medical staff, support measures in valorization of the reference system, promotion of gender equality and inclusion, and recognition of and compliance with social and environmental standards, as well as construction and equipment planning of the satellite hospitals, and training and education in project management in the first year of the operational phase. Likewise, the institutional conditions for qualitatively appropriate modern obstetric and neonatal care will be created.

rows.reporting: UK - Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC)
rows.title: NIHR Global Health Research Group on Nepal Injury Research
rows.description: 1) Engaging with stakeholders to understand their perspectives, awareness of the problem and priorities for change, and enable effective uptake of research. 2) Evidence through data will develop and pilot a hospital based injury surveillance system and explore the feasibility of collecting information on injuries from rural health posts. The Group will understand how injuries happened through interview, focus group, survey and verbal autopsy methods. 3) Effective and cost effective interventions will be developed and tested, adapting proven interventions from other LMICs where possible, and creating new interventions where necessary. Existing knowledge suggests that priority areas are likely to be road injuries, home injuries and the first response to trauma. 4) Enhancing capacity and enabling sustainability. Nepal has a limited and fragmented injury research capacity. The Group will establish partnerships to reduce isolation of researchers, and identify and meet their training needs to enable them to generate internationally excellent research. The Group will work with the Government of Nepal, academic institutions, NGOs and the WHO to plan the sustainability of the research centre in the long term.

rows.description: The goal of the fund is to support the implementation of the Nepal UN Development Assistance Framework (2018-2022), the four pillars of the UNDAF are Inclusive Economic Growth; Social Development; Disaster Risk Reduction, Climate Change Adaptation and Reconstruction; and Governance, Rule of Law and Human Rights.

rows.reporting: UK - Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC)
rows.title: A ‘safe systems’ approach for enabling traffic injury prevention in Nepal (SAFETrIP Nepal)
rows.description:
The objectives of our four work packages are:

Work Package 1:
• To identify existing policies and programmes at local, provincial and national levels of government in Nepal that can be linked to evidence-based recommendations for safer road systems
• To understand the current reach and implementation of these policies and programmes
• To identify the barriers and enablers to further implementation of these policies and programmes
• To develop a toolkit for those working at local and provincial government level to help them improve road safety

Work Package 2:
• To identify the factors that influence how bus transport is currently provided in Nepal, and how those factors affect bus safety
• To establish how these factors affect the current provision of bus transport and which factors can best be used as levers towards safer buses and bus services
• To identify which established, cost-effective, interventions are needed to shift current practice and policy
• To generate agreement across sectors on recommendations to make bus travel safer

Work Package 3:
• To determine the health, social and economic impacts of being injured in a road traffic crash in Nepal.
• To understand the facilitators and barriers to optimal handover of road traffic injured patients from prehospital carers to hospital emergency department staff
• To establish stakeholder agreement on a best practice care pathway for road traffic injured patients to be used in hospital emergency departments

Work Package 4:
• To deliver five, seven-week online good data governance courses between 2023 and 2026
• To develop and make available training materials that enable the safe collection, use and storage of research data
• To strengthen the capacity for peer-to-peer learning on good data governance within and between low and middle-income countries.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening quality infrastructure for sustainable economic development
rows.description:
Despite a continuous economic growth, Nepal is still one of the least developed countries worldwide and faces high barriers to the participation in international trade. People show expandable trust in the safety of domestically produced goods and food.
This project will help Nepali quality infrastructure stakeholders to offer notedly more demand-oriented services. These are elemental for social and economic development, consumer protection and access to international markets.
For this purpose, the Nepal Bureau of Standards and Metrology shall expand their supply of metrological and accreditation services. The Department of Food Technology and Quality Control’s capacity to analyse pesticides shall be enhanced. Private pharmaceutical and dairy associations shall offer more quality assuring services for their members. To achieve this, the project mainly provides training and capacity building. Additionally, measuring instruments for relevant applications will be procured.
All users of quality assurance services make up the project’s target group. This includes industrial and trade businesses as well as the public sector. They shall be reached through cooperation with the Nepal Bureau of Standards and Metrology as well as further public bodies for food control and industry associations.
The project primarily operates in Nepal’s capital Kathmandu. Furthermore, decentralised public laboratories and dairies in selected provinces are supported.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Support to the Tax Administration III
rows.description:
Context: 

For ensuring sustainable social and economic development, it is essential to generate adequate domestic resources. This is particularly true for a state like Nepal, which has endured a serious economic fall-out from the covid pandemic. Nepal has therefore developed a roadmap to improve domestic resource mobilisation capacity, focusing on both tax and non-tax revenue sources. Through thisa mix of policy and administrative measures, Nepal aims to increase its revenuetax to GDP ratio to 30 percent of gross domestic product (GDP) by 2030. The timing of these reforms gain further significance in view of Nepal’s planned graduation from the Least Developed Country (LDC) status in 2026.

Objective: 

Nepal’s tax system is modernized to align with the country’s long-term objective of being a financially stable and investment friendly country.

Approach: 

To contribute to Nepal’s objectives, the RAS project is designed to strengthen the tax administration in Nepal.
RAS capacitates the Inland Revenue Department (IRD) through digital tools and enhanced audit skills. Thereby the project improves the efficiency as well as equitability of the tax system, and contributes to enhancing voluntary tax compliance by facilitating and raising awareness among taxpayers.
The project cooperates with actors at central and subnational level and provides advisory services to the revenue authorities in three areas:
-Strengthening of IT systems at the federal tax authority to improve compliance with taxpayer’s obligations
- Enhancing the audit capacity of the tax authority and introduction of new tools and methodologies
- Improving taxpayer services to reduce compliance costs

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency Programme
rows.description:
Context: 

The government of Nepal seeks to provide reliable, affordable energy to support the economic growth of the country. However, much of Nepal’s renewable energy resources remain untapped and the country still suffers from an energy deficit. Almost two milllion people do not have access to modern energy services.
Furthermore, the country does not use its available energy resources efficiently. Thus, Nepal remains highly dependent on imports of coal and fossil fuels which increases its national trade deficit and greenhouse gas emissions.

Objective: 

Regulatory, institutional and private-sector conditions in Nepal are enhanced in order to promote and implement renewable energies and energy efficiency.

Approach: 

The government of Nepal promotes renewable energies (RE) and energy efficiency (EE) for a positive socio-economic development in the country. The project, co-financed by EU, includes the following key activities:
1. Identifying potentials to conserve energy and to reduce emissions in industries and enterprises in Nepal,  mobilising private sector investment in energy efficiency measures.
2. Supporting women and disadvantaged groups in enhancing their capacity to participate in energy-related decision making processes at subnational level and  in the provision of improved modern energy services to citizens.
3. Supporting the necessary preconditions to promote the private sector in providing energy services through renewable sources to people living in Nepal. The steps include setting up regulatory, financial, and institutional frameworks.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Improvement of Mother and Child Care in Urban Areas - Paropakar Maternity and Womens Hospital Kathmandu II
rows.description: The module aims to improve access to and increase utilization of quality medical care for pregnant women and newborns at the selected satellite hospitals in the Kathmandu Valley, while reducing the burden on and specializing the Paropakar Maternity and Womens Hospital (Paropakar) Kathmandu. Additionally, the module aims to strengthen gender equality and empower women in leadership, management, service delivery and user groups in the catchment area of the satellite hospitals. The recipient is the Government of the Federal Democratic Republic of Nepal. The project executing agency, in addition to the Nepalese Ministry of Health, is Paropakar in Kathmandu. The direct target group is the population living in the Kathmandu Valley, especially women and children. The project measures include the construction of a new satellite hospital in Chandragiri, in the far west of the Kathmandu Valley. The location of the satellite hospital is in the immediate periphery of the Kathmandu Valley, in order to relieve Paropakar of mainly normal and low-risk births and to alleviate existing bottlenecks in mother-child care. The project will also finance necessary medical equipment and the digital connection and networking between Paropakar and the satellite hospital. Measures to promote energy efficiency will also be implemented. A complementary measure is planned to train the executing agencys managerial and executive staff (Paropakar and satellite hospitals), especially in the management, handling, operation and maintenance of financed measures and environmental and social standards.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Support to Gender Equality and Social Inclusion in the Health Sector (S2GESI)
rows.description:
Context: 

The German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) has supported health and social protection system development in Nepal since 1994. Significant progress has been made since the civil war ended in 2006, with notable reductions in maternal and infant mortality. However, societal gender stereotypes and discrimination remain pervasive, posing multiple challenges in the health sector. Inequalities in health access, coupled with corruption risks, deepen existing social tensions. Addressing these challenges requires enhancing institutional mechanisms, empowering vulnerable populations, and tailoring health services and insurance schemes to their needs, aiming to reduce disparities and improve overall health outcomes.

Objective: 

The availability and accessibility of gender responsive health services and social protection provision for women and marginalized groups have improved at both the national and local levels.

Approach: 

S2GESI supports the Ministry of Health and Population (MoHP) and selected municipalities in two provinces in implementing the National Strategy of the Ministry of Health and Population on Gender Equality and Social Inclusion (GESI). It aims to make gender-responsive health services available and accessible for women and marginalized groups at both national and local levels. It focuses on three key areas:
a) mainstreaming of the GESI strategy of the MoHP in municipal health management through demand-driven sector planning for gender-sensitive health services provisions.
b) increasing health insurance coverage and access to good quality health services for wom-en and marginalized groups, facilitated by improved enrolment strategies and electronic birth registration to address the gender gap,
c) implementing approaches to lower supply-side barriers to access health services, including gender-responsive action plans to increase utilisation of good quality health care services by marginalized and vulnerable population

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Support to the Health Sector Strategy II in Nepal
rows.description:
Context: 

The German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) has sup-ported Nepal’s health sector development since 1994. Nepal’s social health security schemes, introduced in recent years, remain underdeveloped, leaving citizens inadequately protected and perpetuating inequities in healthcare access. Moreover, systemic deficits hinder modernization, particularly in digitalization, crucial for im-proving health service delivery.

Objective: 

The institutional capacities of state actors in the health system, in particular the Ministry of Health and Population, the Health Insurance Board and selected municipal health authorities in two provinces, to implement the Nepal Health Sector Strategic Plan have been enhanced.

Approach: 

S2HSS II aims to strengthen the abilities of key actors in the health sector, particularly the Ministry of Health and Population (MoHP), the Health Insurance Board (HIB), and selected sub-national health authorities in two provinces, to provide equitable and quality healthcare services for everyone. Building on past achievements, it focuses on three main areas:
a) Strengthening organizational management and improving the financial sustainability of na-tional health insurance program, that involves supporting HIB to develop strategies for sus-tainable financing and efficient claims management, as a foundation to increase insurance coverage among poor and vulnerable populations.
b) Promoting evidence-based health management through data-driven decision-making and citizen participation, leading to efficient sectoral planning at sub national level and
c) Strengthening digital health information systems by establishing standards, ensuring in-teroperability and allowing efficient data exchange leading to better data-driven decision-making, reduced costs, and enhanced public health outcomes.

To achieve the goals, home visits, trainings, teacher in-service trainings are conducted. Networking and lobbying also play a major role, as well as the support of school guardianship associations.

(agroecology and food sovereignty) in existing and new networks.

By raising awareness and providing access to information and justice, improving skills for the international and domestic labor market, tracking undocumented migration, and building structures and institutions, the project contributes to promoting safe migration and combating the negative effects of human trafficking.
