rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Women in climate resilient agri-systems for enhanced nutrition, Nepal
rows.description: The overall objective of this project is that marginalised citizens of the mountainous District Achham have enhanced their food and nutrition security, climate adaption of their farming system and enjoy a dignified life. The project comprises of two municipalities with the objective to improve the food and nutrition situation of 2.500 marginalised households (Approx. 10375 people) with a special focus on female farmers. It is intended that the small holder farming households practice both, a nutrition-sensitive and climate resilient agriculture. Marginalised citizens, in particular women and youths will be strengthened to practice and advocate their rights on food, nutrition and climate change adaption. The targeted two municipalities will be supported in their strategy development on climate resilient agriculture in addition to improved nutrition governance. Three partner organisations (PeaceWin, WAC and Sambikas) together with local CBOs and other civil society stakeholders will advocate their rights on food, nutrition and adaption to climate change.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Civil society for nature-based local agri-food-systems (CS4FS), Nepal
rows.description: The project contributes to the promotion of local agri-food systems through nature-based solutions and policy localisation led by civil society organisations in Madhesh Province, Nepal. The objective of the project is to improve the food security and resilience of 5000 marginalized households (? 26.610 persons) in three municipalities of Dhanusa District. It is intended that 5000 marginalised households have transitioned towards a regenerative agriculture production at the project end, civil society actors from the agricultural sector promote and advocate regenerative agriculture at municipal and provincial level and 3 local governments support policy and programmes in agriculture in line with the agro-ecological principles (municipal/provincial level). 3 organisations (Aasaman, JWAS and PAC) together with local CBOs from the agricultural sector and other civil society stakeholders will work on a multi-stakeholder approach to promote regenerative agriculture. The project provides inclusive dialogues and links up with local government to address small holder farmers needs and their challenges in farming and to find solutions how their needs should be reflected in the local policies and programmes. The project has a participatory and inclusive approach that ensures that the most marginalised small holder farmers join the advocacy dialogues, including female farmers and farmers from the Dalit/ Musahar, muslim and other ethnic minority communities such as Maghi/ Tharu.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Promoting gender-equitable and inclusive socio-economic living conditions for disadvantaged families in the districts of Dhading and Kanchapur, Nepal.
rows.description: The project aims to strengthen 800 families - especially women and children from 27 villages in the districts of Kanchanpur and Dhading - socio-economically through inclusive and gender-equitable integration, pre-vention and employment measures, to be more effectively protected against poverty and marginalization and to be better pre-pared for natural disasters through inclusive disaster risk reduction management. The project perursues an gender-inclusive approach in the components of womens business start-ups, employment and labor market integration, capacity building at village, school and farmers association level by means of equal self-empowerment and networking, as well as water - sanitation and hygiene (WASH), child protection/ education, disaster risk reduction and advocacy.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Breaking Barriers! Improving sexual and reproductive health and rights of adolescent girls in Dolpa
rows.description: Breaking Barriers has two main objectives: 1. to improve the situation of adolescent girls in SRHR, this includes both building knowledge and empowering girls to know and claim their rights and creating a supportive environment through awareness raising and / or technical support in the provision of girl-friendly services with families, village communities, local government, schools and health facilities. 2. to strengthen the capacity of young girls to confidently advocate for their needs (SRHR but also others) in their communities, to gain agency, decision-making power and influence at the local level. In addition, capacity building of the local project executing agency is prioritised in order to increase sustainability.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: RISE Bajura: Resilient, Inclusive, Sustainable Development and Empowerment of Farmers and Vulnerable Households
rows.description: This project employs the Community-Led-Inclusive-Development Approach (CLIDA), a participatory approach focused on empowering target community groups through self-determination and responsibility for project activities. The aim is for 1.600 households to develop sustainable self-help structures within the target group by working closely with communities and focusing on their needs. This includes planning, implementation, observation, and evaluation with active participation from village communities, utilizing various Participatory Rural Appraisal (PRA) tools. The project targets two main objectives: improving living conditions through sustainable agricultural practices and income-generating activities, and enhancing resilience against disasters and climate change impacts. Actions include training in climate-smart agriculture, sustainable livestock farming, disaster preparedness, and climate change adaptation. Additionally, preparatory measures like assessments and various training sessions are carried out to enhance the knowledge and skills of the target groups, thereby sustainably improving their living conditions and resilience.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: SDG 4, Just Education: Ensure equal access to education and opportunities for children and young people from remote communities in Bajura Nepal.
rows.description: In Nepal, recent federal restructuring coincides with educational challenges: while primary school enrollment is high (91%), secondary rates are low (38.9%). Access to education remains limited, depriving children of prospects. Poverty, unemployment, and unproductive agriculture plague project communities, hindering Nepals development. In Bajura district, despite a 97% enrollment rate, learning outcomes for grade 8 students range from 41-46%. This project aims to address these disparities by providing equitable education and development opportunities, aligning with SDG 4. The goal is to ensure 10,000 children and 2,100 youth in 13 communities have equal access to quality education and future opportunities. Subgoals include establishing monitoring mechanisms, enhancing schooling systems, and strengthening rural economies to reduce vulnerability among children and youth. Structural and social sustainability will be achieved through community involvement and the establishment of Civil Society Organizations.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening rights and improving social inclusion for disadvantaged population groups in western Nepal
rows.description: Measure packages 1.1 Improved equality for people affected by leprosy through awareness raising and education 1.2 Capacity building for early detection of leprosy and prevention of disabilities 2.1 Improved equality for people with disabilities through awareness raising and education 2.2 Strengthen ODPs, self-help groups for PWDs and relevant government agencies 3.1 Local communities are better informed about disaster risks and disaster risk reduction measures 3.2 Local Disaster Management Committees (LDMCs) are prepared and able to respond effectively in case of disasters

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Improving Mental Health and Psychosocial Wellbeing among vulnerable communities in Nuwakot and Rasuwa, Nepal
rows.description: The aim of the project is to improve the mental health and psychosocial well-being of people in vulnerable situations in the districts of Nuwakot and Rasuwa. This is achieved by empowering and enabling the target group, educating their environment about mental health and lobbying at government institutions to implement existing programs and services for mental health and psychosocial well-being.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Healthy Children - Healthy Communities II Participatory development of healthy livelihoods in schools and village communities in rural Nepal
rows.description: The project aims to improve the health and living conditions as well as the school environment of the local population from remote, marginalised village communities and comprises four sub-objectives. The first sub-objective includes measures for village community development. Here, development and action plans are prepared in a participatory manner and adapted to the individual needs of each village community and school with a focus on WASH. The second sub-objective includes activities for construction and repair of WASH infrastructure within the village communities and at the schools. The third sub-goal focusses on improving health knowledge and healthy behaviour. To this end, various training programmes in the areas of WASH, menstrual hygiene, nutrition, first aid, etc. are organized. As part of this, the target group is also provided with materials (seeds, construction materials for latrines, reusable sanitary pads, etc.) so that knowledge can be put into practice. In addition, social structures such as womens groups in particular are strengthened in their organisational form and trained on the above-mentioned topics so that they can act as strong multipliers. The fourth sub-goal includes event formats for sustainable capacity development, networking and advocacy between the village communities and in relation to local government levels.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Revenue sector reform for sustainable development
rows.description: Nepals tax administration and revenue policy have created regulatory and technical conditions for sustainable economic development.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Keine englische Projektbezeichnung!
rows.description:
Context: 

A major challenge for the Nepalese economy, which is largely based on agriculture, is the lack of jobs for young people and the fact that many men are leaving the country to find work. The women who remain have a heavy workload and account for a large proportion of agricultural workers. However, they face multiple forms of discrimination and have limited access to better paid jobs in the agricultural sector.
At the same time, the effects of climate change pose a risk to income from agriculture.

Objective: 

Economic development in Nepal is more gender equitable and more climate resilient.

Approach: 

The project works with Nepalese and international partners such as local community and district administrations to improve the conditions for gender-equitable and climate-resilient economic development. The aim is to support the establishment of companies, the further development and marketing of products, and the economic participation of women.
There are three mains fields of activity:
1. Expanding access to services such as new processing strategies and digital services for entrepreneurs to support the development of existing or newly established companies, particularly women-led companies.
2. Promoting partnerships with the private sector through, for example, community projects with trading companies in order to provide economic support for women in particular.
3. Expanding gender-equitable and climate-resilient economic development through local government, for example through the implementation of action plans for local economic development with a focus on gender-equitable and climate-resilient business promotion.
The project works primarily in the provinces of Karnali and Sudurpaschim. It is designed to have an impact in other regions of Nepal, such as Lumbini, Koshi and Kathmandu, and internationally in the Nepalese export sector, for example.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Building a safety net to improve the health of mothers and newborns in Nepal
rows.description: In the project country, 1,500 mothers and 12,000 newborns die every year from preventable causes. The aim of this project is therefore to ensure that pregnant women and their newborns receive high-quality medical care and thus reduce the risk of complications during pregnancy, labour and delivery.  reduce the risk of complications during pregnancy, labour and delivery. To this end, obstetricians and healthcare professionals are being trained and further educated, simulation-based training rooms are being set up and numerous birthing centres are being upgraded to meet the minimum standard.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening the social security system and improving the livelihoods of marginalised groups in four provinces of Nepal
rows.description: The majority of Nepalese are not covered by social security and are largely on their own in the event of illness, unemployment, natural disasters or other risks, unemployment, natural disasters or other risks. The aim is to work closely with the local government and relevant stakeholders and to promote the effective implementation and improvement of current social protection programmes and initiatives through exchange, coordination, workshops, public relations and lobbying. The primary target groups are marginalised population groups in Nepal (children, young people, women, senior citizens...)

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Empowering women, children and young people and promoting gender equality in Nepal
rows.description: The project area is a region with extremely patriarchal social structures in which girls and women have hardly any opportunities to exercise their constitutional rights. There is an urgent need in this region to protect women and children from abuse, violence and human trafficking. Their access to education, healthcare, economic opportunities and participation in political decision-making processes is limited. The project strengthens the rights of women and girls through health and income-generating measures, the provision of shelters, networking, public relations work and legal advice, among

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Prevention of sexual exploitation, human trafficking and unsafe migration in Nepal
rows.description: Nepal is the country of origin of many forced prostitutes who end up in the brothels of Indias major cities or are sold as domestic servants to the Gulf States or Malaysia, where they are often sexually exploited. The reasons that contribute to human trafficking are extreme poverty, illiteracy, unemployment, domestic and other forms of violence, social discrimination and the lack of political will to take action against these abuses. Survivors of human trafficking receive assistance and support so that they can lead a life in dignity again. Furthermore, girls and women at risk, their families

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Empowering the Landless Womens Association to participate in democratic processes in Nepal
rows.description: There are laws and policies in Nepal that have been enacted by the government in favour of the landless and informal settlers. However, there are gaps in implementation, especially for marginalised women. The project supports landless marginalised women in implementing and enforcing existing land and housing laws in Nepal. Among other things, these women are educated about the current legal situation, receive training to lead political movements, work with actors at government level and lobby. These women are also empowered to improve their living conditions through income-generating measures

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: International networking of the Nepal Dialogue Forum on Human Rights and Peace in Nepal
rows.description: Despite the countrys emergency situation, Nepals situation receives little international attention. Especially in the current geopolitical situation, there is a risk that attention will be limited to countries affected by wars or violent conflicts. The project aims to strengthen the scope for action of civil society actors in Nepal in order to increase pressure on the Nepalese government at international level to address the social problems, marginalisation and the human rights situation in Nepal. The target group is not only civil society actors, but also political decision-makers in Germa

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Promoting Right to Food and Food Sovereignty through Community-led Agro-Ecology Practices
rows.description: This project aims to tackle problems such as malnutrition and undernourishment by realizing the right to food and food security combined with the implementation of agroecological practices and thus contribute to the achievement of SDG 2. The goals are to be achieved together with community groups, civil society organizations and government representatives. The direct target group, which primarily consists of marginalized population groups and groups affected by violations of the right to food, should also learn to assert their rights.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Fostering Civic Engagement for a Peaceful and Climate Friendly Governance
rows.description: The project strengthens the capacities of all levels of society for peaceful conflict resolution. The aim is to strengthen citizens commitment and voices in favour of social peace and environmental sustainability. Existing governance conflicts are to be resolved in moderated dialogues with various stakeholders. The project includes training measures for civil society as well as advocacy and lobbying measures at local, provincial and national level for peaceful governance and climate justice.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Scaling up climate-resilient agriculture for sustainable livelihood of smallholder farmers, continuation
rows.description: The aims of the project are to combat multidimensional poverty and contribute to the food security of farmers. Against the backdrop of the effects of climate change, the aim is to increase the income of smallholder farmers through climate-resilient agroecological farming methods and to incorporate agroecology into government plans, programs and regulations. One focus is on strengthening the capacity of women in agriculture, e.g. through women-friendly agricultural equipment and awareness-raising on agroecology for government representatives.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening Governance for Development and Social Justice of disadvantaged women, continuation
rows.description: Women who belong to marginalised population groups are exposed to multiple forms of discrimination. The objective of the project is to achieve their full social and political participation in society. To this end, a movement is to be created that protects and promotes the rights of disadvantaged and vulnerable women. The measures therefore amongst other include the targeted strengthening of womens groups and networks and strategic advocacy work.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Built Resilient Livelihoods of Vulnerable People, continuation
rows.description: Discrimination based on caste, gender and/or disability as well as food and livelihood insecurity are particularly pronounced in the project areas. The project aims to build and maintain the livelihoods of poor and marginalized communities. Through campaigning, trainings and material support, the project aims to build the capacity and empowerment of local community groups. The project contributes to the fight against hunger, poverty and discrimination and to the development of self-help structures in rural communities.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Mobilization of the community for high-quality education in Solukhumbu district
rows.description: In two rural communities in the Himalayas, local communities and governments are being supported in establishing a high-quality, inclusive and child-friendly education system. Through capacity building and mobilisation of key stakeholders, such as teachers, adolescents, parents and local authorities, sustainable structures are being developed for the provision of resources and the monitoring of educational quality.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Garima - Dignity for Women II - end Chhaupadi: safe menstruation instead of stigmatisation and access to reproductive health in Western Nepal
rows.description: The project will improve the sexual and reproductive health (SRH) of women and girls through health promotion, WASH and the involvement of all stakeholders. Dangerous discriminatory practices related to menstruation (Chhaupadi) will be reduced. As a result, women and girls can sleep in their homes and receive a normal diet, they learn hygienic methods for menstruation, and girls attend regular classes. By mobilising local health workers, traditional healers, teachers, gatekeepers and local administrations, the right to safe menstruation is strengthened.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Food Security and Resilient Livelihoods with Focus on Earthquake Affected Families through Civil Society Strengthening (SUSTAIN)
rows.description: The project aims to support 2,500 smallholder households in the districts of Jajarkot and Rukum West in western Nepal to sustainably secure their livelihoods and improve their nutrition and income situation by strengthening civil society structures (self-help groups, CBOs) and local administrative and government capacities. The target group includes earthquake-affected households and marginalised groups (women, youth, Dalits, Janajatis, people with disabilities). The project promotes climate-smart agricultural practices and access to quality inputs. By supporting sustainable entrepreneurial activities (green enterprises), the project creates new income opportunities for marginalised groups (women, youth) through local value creation. To consolidate these efforts, the project is working with civil society organisations to prioritise climate-resilient agricultural and economic development in local development plans and policy strategies, and to build relevant administrative and institutional capacity. The project follows a multi-stakeholder approach, collaborating with long-standing (RRN) and new (MIC, FTG) national partner organisations, CBOs, local governments and other civil society actors.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Empowering women and community-based organizations of Western Mountain for a resilient local food system (EMPOWER)
rows.description: The proposed project aims to strengthen resilient local food systems by empowering women smallholders, marginalized communities, and civil society actors in Budhinanda Municipality (Bajura district, Sudurpaschim province) and Khatyad Municipality (Mugu district, Karnali province). Over a period of 44 months (November 2025 - June 2029), the project will directly benefit 1,600 smallholder farming households (3,200 individu-als), primarily women-headed, by improving their food, nutrition, and income security through the adoption of agroecological farming practices. The project will also work with elected representatives, extension workers from local and provincial govern-ments, and community-based organizations, including producer groups and resource user associations, to build institutional capacities for sustainable agricultural govern-ance. Implemented in partnership with LI-BIRD (Local Initiatives for Biodiversity Re-search and Development), KCDC (Karnali Community Development Centre), and FWEAN (Federation of Women Entrepreneurs Associations of Nepal), the initiative will ensure effective execution, local ownership, and long-term impact.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening resilience in WASH + livelihood of vulnerable communities (esp. women) in Khaptad Channa Rural Municipality, Bajhang District, Nepal
rows.description: The project aims to strengthen the resilience of vulnerable households in Khaptad Channa Rural Municipality, Nepal. It promotes income generation through the development of agricultural value chains, training, cooperative support, and improved market access. Simultaneously, WASH services are enhanced through planning, infrastructure, and awareness campaigns. Special focus is placed on empowering women and marginalized groups to foster sustainable social, economic, and health-related participation.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Youth empowerment through integrated education and training for resilience and local prospects in Kathmandu Valley
rows.description: The project addresses the challenges of climate change and the lack of local livelihoods for the local youth in Kathmandu Valley. It aims to empower youth and promote sustainable, enhanced climate resilience and local livelihood opportunities in Kathmandu Valley. Through a regular offer of education and awareness programs, children and youth enhance their understanding of environmental and climate related risks and gain capacities to face these challenges with suitable adaptation and response measures. A dedicated vocational and skills development program, based on local needs and opportunities, supports vulnerable local youth and equips them with relevant technical and business skills to create income and employment opportunities at the local level. The project supports skills training in sustainable tourism and hospitality as an answer to employment and business opportunities in the project area. Capacity development of local civil society actors and close collaboration with local government stakeholders further foster the sustainable development of the area while respecting environmental boundaries and responding to the effects of climate change in the area.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Gender-Friendly Agroforestry Project for Sustainable Livelihoods, Climate Resilience and Adaptation
rows.description: The Gender-Friendly Agroforestry Project is a comprehensive initiative that aims to empower women, enhance sustainable livelihoods, and build climate resilience in the Solukhumbu region. By promoting gender-responsive agroforestry practices, providing infrastructure and resources, and facilitating market access, the project contributes to the socio-economic development of rural communities. Through collaboration with local governments and institutions, the project seeks to institutionalize its achievements and ensure the long-term sustainability of agroforestry initiatives. By addressing climate change challenges and promoting biodiversity conservation, the project paves the way for a more sustainable and resilient future for the region.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Care and support for HIV infected people in Nepal
rows.description: The project aims to improve the living conditions of families affected by HIV/AIDS in the project area. It is aimed at people living with HIV/AIDS and their families.  In order to improve the well-being of those affected, they receive psychosocial and medical support in the form of outpatient or inpatient medical treatment, regular tests, psychosocial counselling, etc. The educational and nutritional opportunities for children infected and affected by HIV are improved by, among other things, supporting school attendance (e.g. school materials) and providing nutritional supplements. The project

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening the social and economic situation of women and children and promoting sustainable agriculture in four districts of Nepal
rows.description: Family-run farms and, in particular, female smallholders often cannot progress beyond subsistence farming in Nepal because productivity is low and access to land and natural resources is poor. The effects of climate change are damaging crops and jeopardising food security. 2,182 women and people from ethnic minorities are receiving support by strengthening the cooperatives run by women in terms of their services, advice and management, and are helping to improve the social and economic well-being of those affected and establish sustainable agriculture in the project region.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Local peace initiatives in Nepal
rows.description: The project aims to promote social peace, strengthen the capability of women and youth and consolidate peace structures at the community level to develop a resilient and inclusive society. This is done by supporting the development of individual capacities of women, youth and marginalised groups, promoting a culture of peace and social harmony through initiatives and spaces and conflict prevention, particularly in the diverse groups and communities in the project area. To this end, various training courses, workshops and training sessions are offered. Regular meetings are held, a steering comm

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Reducing the Culture of Impunity in Nepal
rows.description: The project aims to empower Nepalese actors to fight impunity in Nepal and promote the rule of law. The objectives are to improve access to justice and reparation for victims of human rights violations. In addition, the capacities of local actors providing legal assistance will be strengthened, as will cooperation between them. Furthermore, the Nepalese authorities are taking concrete steps to ensure greater justice and accountability for victims of crimes under international law. The partner organisation will represent victims of international crimes by providing them with access to justice,

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Implementation of a joint project on sustainability and the generation and storage of renewable energies with the municipality of Rainas / Nepal
rows.description: As part of Agenda 2030 and the Global Responsibility and One World theme of Detmolds sustainability strategy, the city of Detmold has entered into a long-term and equal city partnership with the municipality of Rainas in Nepal, specifically on the topic of sustainability. During the visit of the Nepalese delegation to Detmold in December 2023, it was jointly determined that the topics of renewable energy production and use represent a common area of interest in which extensive knowledge could be exchanged and joint projects and implementation steps defined. For example, the municipality of Rainas has a photovoltaic system that is currently unusable due to a technical defect. In the meantime, the exchange has been further intensified and a cooperation project has been set up with Stadtwerke Detmold and the association Elektriker ohne Grenzen e.V. (Electricians without Borders), in which a delegation from the Detmold city administration, Detmold municipal utilities and the association will travel to Rainas to repair the existing photovoltaic system and establish further cost-effective decentralised solutions such as balcony power plants with storage facilities, initially as part of a pilot project.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Capacity building in the area of social inclusion
rows.description: Guided by the values and mission of the organisation and in view of the persistent and long-standing marginalisation of women and various excluded communities, it has been decided to make social inclusion one of the most important cross-cutting themes of the work. A specialist will effectively support the systematic operationalisation of thematic mainstreaming. This includes the development of new tools in the development of projects in this area, detailed reports and comprehensive documentation.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening capacities for womens rights
rows.description: The project country ranks among the lowest in the world in terms of gender equality. The project is actively helping to reduce gender-specific violence. Among other things, women human rights defenders are being trained comprehensively. A specialist is to support the organisation in effectively anchoring new and innovative approaches in the area of mental health and communication. The aim of the project is to develop a comprehensive psychosocial support system and to successfully put it into practical use.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Climate Resilient Communities and Sustainable Livelihood Initiatives for Pro-Poor, continuation
rows.description: The aim of the project is to improve the climate resilience of the most vulnerable communities by promoting entrepreneurial skills in agriculture, climate-resilient agricultural techniques, innovative irrigation methods and forest management through training and workshops. All activities are carried out in cooperation with the local government. Additional activities include work to update the governments climate action plan in order to reduce climate induced vulnerability.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Local Initiatives for Food Security Transformation Project, Continuation
rows.description: The project aims to improve the livelihoods of disadvantaged and marginalised farmers.  Therefore, farming communities are formed and  strengthened with sustainable and resilient food systems and equipped with entrepreneurial skills. In addition, further alternative income opportunities are created and resilience to unexpected shocks will be strengthened. The cooperation with government agencies is crucial in this process and therefore will be integrated and strengthened as well.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Fostering the rights of the people especially victims of human rights violations and abuses, continuation
rows.description: The objectives of the project are that victims of human rights violation and abuse receive access to legal services and their rights to justice and that human rights defenders hold the state accountable for fundamental rights and on issues related to transitional justice. The project works through human rights violations documentation, capacity building, human rights awareness and advocacy and aims to overcome caste-based and ethnic discrimination, focusing on marginalized communities and women.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Youth Empowerment and Eco-Entrepreneurship Project, continuation
rows.description: The aim of the project is to build capacities of young people in remote rural areas who are engaged in community development and eco-entrepreneurship. These young people belong to ethnic groups affected by severe poverty and discrimination. They are trained in eco-friendly agriculture and livestock farming to increase their resilience and income, thus providing them with a sustainable livelihood as an alternative to migration. In addition, the young people use their strengthened capacities to contribute to community development in order to improve the situation for the entire community.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Strengthening the right to food, continuation
rows.description: The aim of the project is to secure the right to food and food security for the most vulnerable communities. Orientation events, consultations, discussions and dialogues with newly formed community groups (especially womens groups) are held for this purpose. Further measures with regard to adaptation to climate change include practical training courses and support with vegetable seeds, etc. Raising awareness among newly elected government representatives and networking among interest groups relevant to the target population are also central components of the project activities.

rows.reporting: Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
rows.title: Changemakers for Sustainable Peace, continuation
rows.description: The project aims to establish community-led peacebuilding mechanisms such as community peace centers, which will be managed by the communities themselves in the long term. Trained community members support local communities in addressing individual and collective problems and finding peaceful solutions. The project places a particular focus on young women. An important component, therefore, is the strengthening of skills and self-esteem among young people, many of whom come from marginalized communities. To address the wide range of psychological problems, many of which are also a result of the armed conflict, psychosocial counselling is being offered.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Strengthening the National Rural Transport Program (SNRTP)- Nepal
rows.description: The Government of Nepal has negotiated with the World Bank for the Project for Strengthening National Rural Transport Program (SNRTP) to enhance all weather rural transport connectivity. This negotiation has been approved by the Executive Directors of the International Development Association (IDA).The bilateral financing agreement are under process This project is follow-on of ongoing Rural Access Improvement and Decentralization Project (RAIDP) under the Department of Local Infrastructure Development and Agricultural Roads (DoLIDAR).The project has given high focus on the maintenance of the rural roads in the project participant districts. SNRTP’s envisaged technical scope includes: (i) institutional strengthening; (ii) Routine and Periodic Maintenance of existing all weather roads and river crossings; (iii) upgrading and rehabilitation of seasonal roads to provide new all weather access; (iv) construction of new river crossing structures. The proposed SNRTP project aligns with the Interim Strategy Note (ISN) for Nepal and the forthcoming World Bank Group Country Partnership Strategy (CPS). Pillar two of the draft CPS specifically highlights improving connectivity throughout Nepal as a key consideration for inclusive economic growth and rural poverty reduction. These aims are consistent with the both of the World Bank’s strategic goals to reduce extreme poverty and increase shared prosperity amongst the bottom 40%. SNRTP aims at improving rural accessibility, is funded by the World Bank (WB) grant. SNRTP will target the 30 districts that previously participated in the Bank-supported Rural Access Improvement and Decentralization Project (RAIDP) plus an additional 3 districts. The project will benefit around 15.7 million people. The project will benefit around 15.7 million people. The project will provide all-weather access to a network of 3,067 km; the average number of beneficiary population per kilometer is around 1,434 person; and most of the beneficiary population live in Terai and valley plains, and 51 percent of the beneficiary population in the hills live within 30 minutes of the road.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Advocacy for Rights and Good Corporate Governance (UNNATI-Inclusive Growth Programme in Nepal)
rows.description: The project aims at improving advocacy for responsible business development including rights and good corporate governance. The project is a sub component 3.2 of the UNNATI- Inclusive Growth Programme in Nepal and has three complementary areas of implementation: • Building capabilities for advocacy and dialogue • Promoting sustainability • Promoting broad public awareness of the importance of responsible business including rights and corporate governance issues An advocacy Challenge Fund will be established and the project will run under the challenge fund model. The project is a sub component 3.2 of the UNNATI-Inclusive Growth Programme in Nepal and has three complementary areas of implementation:

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Strengthening the national rural transport program (SNRTP).Nepal
rows.description: Project Development Objective The overall project development objective is to enhance the availability and reliability of transport connectivity for rural communities in the 3 additional highly earthquake affected districts with 33 SNRTP participating districts. SNRTP – Project Components Component A (institutional strengthening and technical assistance): Component A of SNRTP will fund three categories of activities, including: ¿ Institutional strengthening and technical assistance; ¿ Third party monitoring of physical works under Component B of the project; and ¿ An impact study of selected project interventions. Target districts will receive technical assistance from Component A for: ¿ Updating District Transport Master Plans (DTMPs) and Multi-Year Rolling Maintenance Plans (MYRMP); There will be an opportunity for any district in Nepal to receive limited support from Component A in the following specific areas: (i) assistance to develop or update District Transport Master Plans (DTMPs); and (ii) introduction and operationalization of length-person or similar schemes to undertake routine maintenance on rural roads and bridges. 1.4.2 Component B (Civil Works): Component B will support districts to implement physical works needed for (i) upgrading or rehabilitating rural transport infrastructure; and (ii) maintaining rural transport infrastructure. There will be two funding windows within component B as follows: Window 1 will support routine and periodic maintenance of roads and crossing structures using a simplified output-based disbursement approach that ties a portion of payments to the completion of works. Window 1 will support maintenance of all season roads and crossing structures. Access to these funds will depend on a district’s ability to: ¿ Pass the Minimum Conditions; and ¿ Satisfy a basic set of project-specific criteria. Window 1 funding will disburse on the basis of verified physical outputs achieved. This will require compliance with all safeguards and procurement guidelines. SNRTP districts will co-fund a portion of maintenance costs from their own financial resources to complement Window 1 funding. Within Window 1, SNRTP will support districts to operationalize group-based routine length person schemes for routine maintenance with support from the International Labor Organization (ILO). Window 2 will support road upgrading and or rehabilitation) and new crossing structure construction as needed to provide all-weather connectivity. Roads upgrading and new crossing structure construction under Window 2 must be complementary such that access along the integrated road linkage becomes possible year-round. Investments in road upgrading and new crossing structures may occur in parallel. However, Window 2 will not support upgrading for road linkages without supporting crossing structures. Similarly, districts will not receive Window 2 funds for developing new crossing structures on roads that only provide seasonal access. Component C (Market Infrastructure): Component C will fund investments in market related infrastructure along roads that offer all season connectivity to project districts. A key aim of Component C will be to pilot an approach for linking infrastructure investments in rural transport infrastructure with infrastructure investments in locations that provide economic opportunity.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: From Protocol to Practice: A Bridge to Global Action on Forced Labor (The Bridge project - Nepal)
rows.description: From Protocol to Practice: A Bridge to Global Action on Forced Labor (The Bridge project) The U.S. Department of Labor (USDOL), Bureau of International Labor Affairs (ILAB), has provided funding to the International Labour Organization (ILO) for a project to support global and national efforts aimed at combating forced labor under the 2014 ILO Protocol and Recommendation on Forced Labor. The project will: (1) promote the ratification and implementation of the Protocol and implementation of the Recommendation by countries by providing technical assistance to governments and other key stakeholders, (2) support interventions at the global and country level to advance the campaign to eradicate forced labor, including addressing gaps in legislation, awareness raising campaigns, supply chain monitoring, law enforcement training, best practices on compensation and other remedies, research studies and surveys, and (3) develop and implement direct action measures to prevent forced labor, to protect victims, and to provide them with access to remediation, including compensation for material and physical harm.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Integrated Programme on Fair Recruitment (Nepal component)
rows.description: This global project seeks to contribute to the promotion of fair recruitment initiatives across migration corridors in North Africa, the Middle East and South Asia. The corresponding project strategy is based on a three-pronged approach: 1) establishing fair recruitment corridors to prevent abuses and exploitation of migrant workers; 2) providing migrant workers with access to reliable information and improved services and 3) conducting innovative research and disseminating knowledge on ethical recruitment. These components will be implemented through several pilot initiatives at the global and country level. Target countries for pilot projects proposed in this concept note include Tunisia, Jordan, Nepal and the Philippines. The strategy will apply a multi-stakeholder approach in conjunction with governments, trade unions, employers’ organisations and the media. This integrated project has been developed jointly by the ILO’s Special Action Programme to Combat Forced Labour (FPRW/SAP-FL) and the Labour Migration Branch (MIGRANT).

rows.reporting: International Organization for Migration (IOM)
rows.title: Migration Health Management Information System (MHMIS): Strengthening Capacity on Data Driven Policy and Planning in Nepal
rows.description: This project will support the government of Nepal to introduce a comprehensive database which will serve as a primary reference point for migration health related information collected from all different information management system of the government and non-government institutions.

rows.reporting: International Organization for Migration (IOM)
rows.title: Early and Improved Tuberculosis (TB) Case Detection and Treatment among Migrants and Their Families in Provinces 1, 2 and 3 of Nepal through Public Private Mix (PPM) Approach
rows.description: The objectives of the project are to improve early TB case detection by improving the capacity of private HACs, improve access to rapid TB diagnostics, and engage and empower women in decision making for screening and testing for definitive TB detection. In addition, migrants with other health issues such as positive HIV results, diabetes and female migrants with gynecological problems will further linked with public health system.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Towards fair and sustainable global supply chains: Promoting formalization and decent work for invisible workers in South Asia
rows.description: The ILO/Japan project “Towards fair and sustainable global supply chains: Promoting formalization and decent work for invisible workers in South Asia” aims to contribute to the development of ethical and sustainable global supply chains. The project is geared towards promoting decent work in global supply chains – specifically for those workers who are engaged at the lower tiers. They are typically home-based workers, subcontractors, and workers in medium and small enterprises in the informal economy in South Asia. Towards the upper-tiers of global supply chains see, larger and more formal enterprise have linkages with large, medium-sized and small enterprises engaged in the production of goods in both the formal and informal economies. Due to competitive markets and demands for speedy delivery, these enterprises often adopt highly flexible production and work patterns. This results in informal working arrangements, piece-rate production, home-based work and non-standard forms of employment. This is the second phase of the project titled “Way out of Informality in South Asia” which has been implemented in Bangladesh, India and Nepal during 2012-2016. In close cooperation with government, employers’ and workers’ organizations, international brand companies, and other key stakeholders such as NGOs and networks working to promote decent work in global supply chains, the project works at the policy, institutional and community levels and implement key interventions in the areas of research, awareness raising and capacity building, knowledge sharing, social dialogue, and policy advocacy to contribute to the development of ethical and sustainable global supply chains. The project is implemented mainly in India and Nepal, and good practices and lessons from the project are shared with other South Asian countries such as Bangladesh, Pakistan and Sri Lanka.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: ILO Technical Assistance - Skills for Employment Programme
rows.description: This is a technical and financial proposal for the ILO’s Technical Assistance component of the Nepal country office of the United Kingdom's Department for International Development (DFID)'s Skills for Employment Programme (SEP). SEP aims to provide Nepali workers with more productive domestic employment opportunities by filling skill gaps in both formal and informal sectors, with a focus on sectors that are instrumental for transformational economic growth in Nepal. SEP will also increase incomes for migrant workers (through reduced cost of migration and increased incomes in employment) and facilitate higher savings and more productive investment of remittances. The overall aim of the programme is to support domestic employment creation and reduce long-term dependency on migration, whilst recognising the importance of migration as a source of work for Nepali workers. Promoting employment-centric and inclusive growth through skill development, increasing formal sector employment, and maximising the development impact of labour migration are cornerstones of the ILO's Decent Work Country Programme for Nepal (2013-2017). Some achievements as part of this work include the formulation of the Labour Law, the Social Protection Act and the National Employment Policy, as well as technical support provided to the Government to conduct Labour Force Surveys. On migration, the ILO in Nepal is providing technical assistance to Government through labour migration projects, including the DFID-funded Work in Freedom (WiF) programme, and the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC)-funded Integrated Programme on Fair Recruitment (FAIR). The ILO is currently piloting an intervention to design and operationalize a system for skills and prior-learning recognition for Nepali returnee migrant workers employed in construction sectors in receiving countries. These areas of work are also a crucial aspect of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. Goal 8 on decent work includes specific indicators on creating policies for job creation, formalisation, the promotion of safe employment for migrants and the eradication of forced labour, modern slavery and trafficking. The Technical Assistance component of SEP aims to achieve results in the above areas of work by supporting the Government of Nepal in creating partnerships with key stakeholders, increasing coordination, developing research and evidence, and implementing relevant policies, to create domestic jobs and enable a higher development impact from migration.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: ILO-DFID Partnership Programme on Fair Recruitment and Decent Work for Women Migrant Workers in South Asia and the Middle East -Phase II
rows.description: The ILO-DFID partnership programme is a five-year, inter-regional development cooperation programme addressing forced labour. It constitutes a second phase of the previous Work in Freedom Programme (2013-2018) The overall aim of the programme is to reduce vulnerability to trafficking and forced labour of women and girls across migration pathways leading to the care work and textiles, clothing, leather and footwear industries of South Asia and Arab States. The project will contribute towards bringing about a sustained situation where women and girls in South Asia and Arab States are less vulnerable to trafficking and forced labour in emerging sectors of migrant employment. The programme will address key drivers and vulnerabilities of human trafficking, such as (a) gender and other forms of discrimination, distress migration and poor working and living conditions, through an integrated prevention strategy of (1) targeted social protection and empowerment, (2) fair recruitment practices and (3) evidence based policy advocacy for decent work options. At least 350,000 women and girls will be reached at source and destination in India, Nepal, and Bangladesh, Oman, Bahrein, Lebanon and Jordan . If policy recommendations are implemented, the programme could indirectly reach over one million. Key stakeholders will include governments, social partners, the private sector, NGOs and importantly, the voice and participation of women migrant workers themselves.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Migrant Rights and Decent Work (MiRiDeW)
rows.description: The overall goal of the project is to enable democratic institutions of Nepal to better protect Migrants (male, females and discriminated groups) and their families, as well as to ensure they benefit from decent working conditions abroad. This is to be achieved by strengthening the support systems of the Government of Nepal in in countries of destinations and to build its capacity to play an active role in regional and global fora on migration issues, as well as to reflect back its commitments in national policies and programs. The project further promotes new destinations and new sectors in existing destinations for low-skilled Nepalese workers. The project is implemented in collaboration with the Nepalese Government, civil society organizations and the private sector.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Integrated Programme on Fair Recruitment (FAIR) - Phase II - Nepal component
rows.description: This global project seeks to consolidate and expand the achievements produced by ILO’s FAIR I project, through the upscaling of the pilots tested under FAIR I as well as the implementation of new fair recruitment interventions across migration corridors in North and West Africa, the Middle East and South and South-East Asia. The project strategy is based on a three-pronged approach: 1) implementing fair recruitment processes in selected migration corridors and sectors, 2) providing reliable information, improved services including facilitating access to justice, to migrant workers in the recruitment process and 3) producing and disseminating global knowledge and guidance about fair recruitment, including through the media.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Constituents and stakeholders, provincial, and local levels have promoted more and better jobs, especially for young women, men and disadvantaged groups
rows.description: This project aims at promoting more and better jobs in Nepal through the development of Micro-, Small- and Medium-Sized Enterprises (MSMEs). The project is a follow-up to the Enabling Environment for Sustainable Enterprises assessment, conducted in 2017 with ILO support and full participation of ILO tripartite constituents and key stakeholders of the MSME sector. It focuses on the implementation of the assessment recommendations prioritized by the tripartite constituents and enterprises operating in four sectors – agro-based industries, tourism, ICT and manufacturing. The project interventions are implemented at federal, provincial and municipality levels in line with their specific roles and responsibilities for promoting sustainable enterprises. It strengths the capacity of constituents, in particular the private sector, to engage in advocacy and social dialogue with high-level government officials and parliamentarians so as to create a conducive environment for sustainable enterprises and improve the policies, institutions and services relating to MSMEs. Interventions create opportunities for ILO to strengthen outreach to and undertake joint work with development partners in Nepal. The project contributes to the Outcome 1.2 of the DWCP (2018-2022) and to Outcome 1 of UNDAF (2018-2022) for Nepal. It further contributes to the country’s efforts to advance towards the attainment of SDG targets 8.3 (Promote development-oriented policies that support productive activities, decent job creation, entrepreneurship, creativity and innovation, and encourage the formalization and growth of micro-, small- and medium-sized enterprises, including through access to financial services),

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Gender Responsive Recovery for Sustainable Peace (GRRSP)
rows.description: The project will provide increased access of conflict affected women to psycho-social services; Increased community awareness on issues of GBV, human rights, including workers’rights and gender equality; Enhanced capacity of conflict affected women on organizational, leadership and social harmonization; Improved knowledge and skills of conflict affected women and their families for improving agricultural productivity; Enhanced technical and entrepreneurial skills of conflict affected women and their families; Increased capacity of local bodies and concerned service delivery agencies for gender responsive planning and implementation mechanisms. Key Activities will include: provision of psycho-social support; capacity development of LPCs and GEWG; public awareness on gender issues; development of agricultural technical know-how and skills for target groups; implementation of FFS; establishment of local resource centers; development of entrepreneurial and vocational skills of target group; facilitation of linkages with local bodies, technical and business service providers and LPCs; sensitization and training for local bodies and service providers on gender responsive planning and implementation mechanisms.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Towards Achieving the Elimination of the Worst Forms of Child Labour as Priority (ACHIEVE)
rows.description: Child labour remains a persistent problem in Nepal. Although the national estimates show that child labour is decreasing in Nepal with an average of 1,00,000 every year, Nepal still has 1.6 million children as child labourers out of which 621,000 children are working in the worst forms of child labour. The Child Labour Phase I Project funded by the Embassy of Denmark on ‘Support to the Implementation of Nepal’s National Master Plan on the Elimination of Child Labour in Nepal”(August 2011-May 2013) focused on developing national level capacities of the Government and the social partners and improving the policy environment. But for greater impact, the efforts initiated by the Phase I of the Project need to be scaled up and sustained. It is in view of the challenges and the need to promote the child labour issues and concerns, the ACHIEVE Project supported by the Embassy of Demark, is designed at a request of the Ministry of Labour and Employment (MoLE) and the social partners. The three year Project aims to establish collaboration and partnerships with a wide range of governmental and non-governmental agencies, in particular the MoLE and social partners. The work with the ILO Constituents and the relevant stakeholders will result in two major outcomes; i) Child labour free communities created through replicable and scalable models, and ii) Policy environment and institutional capacities strengthened at the central and district levels and across a wide range of institutions that can contribute towards child labour elimination.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Occupational Safety and Health Development in Nepal (SHIELD) Project

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Strengthening the National Rural Transport Program (SNRTP)- Nepal
rows.description: The Government of Nepal has negotiated with the World Bank for the Project for Strengthening National Rural Transport Program (SNRTP) to enhance all weather rural transport connectivity. This negotiation has been approved by the Executive Directors of the International Development Association (IDA).The bilateral financing agreement are under process This project is follow-on of ongoing Rural Access Improvement and Decentralization Project (RAIDP) under the Department of Local Infrastructure Development and Agricultural Roads (DoLIDAR).The project has given high focus on the maintenance of the rural roads in the project participant districts. SNRTP’s envisaged technical scope includes: (i) institutional strengthening; (ii) Routine and Periodic Maintenance of existing all weather roads and river crossings; (iii) upgrading and rehabilitation of seasonal roads to provide new all weather access; (iv) construction of new river crossing structures. The proposed SNRTP project aligns with the Interim Strategy Note (ISN) for Nepal and the forthcoming World Bank Group Country Partnership Strategy (CPS). Pillar two of the draft CPS specifically highlights improving connectivity throughout Nepal as a key consideration for inclusive economic growth and rural poverty reduction. These aims are consistent with the both of the World Bank’s strategic goals to reduce extreme poverty and increase shared prosperity amongst the bottom 40%. SNRTP aims at improving rural accessibility, is funded by the World Bank (WB) grant. SNRTP will target the 30 districts that previously participated in the Bank-supported Rural Access Improvement and Decentralization Project (RAIDP) plus an additional 3 districts. The project will benefit around 15.7 million people. The project will benefit around 15.7 million people. The project will provide all-weather access to a network of 3,067 km; the average number of beneficiary population per kilometer is around 1,434 person; and most of the beneficiary population live in Terai and valley plains, and 51 percent of the beneficiary population in the hills live within 30 minutes of the road.

rows.description: The project Trade Unions for Social Justice – phase II will assist the labour movement to overcome such obstacles through outputs aimed at empowering workers’ organization through capacity building and at the same time increasing their influence on policies pertaining to labour and rights. The implementing partners will be national centre trade unions, and through them supporting partnerships with other relevant stakeholders. When applicable, the implementation will seek to bring the various trade unions together in platforms, allowing the activities to have positive side effects through effectively building and maintaining arenas of confidence building within the labour movement. The Immediate Objectives are: I.O.1: Improved capacity of national trade unions in the areas of collective bargaining, trade union services, gender equality and participation in the DWCPs; I.O.2: Strengthened national trade union platforms and regional networks to enhance union influence on national policies.

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Actuarial Studies for the Establishment of Social Security Schemes (ASSESS) : ILO Technical Assistance to the Social Security Fund in Nepal
rows.description: The strategy of the project is to ensure good financial governance of the newly to be created social security schemes to be implemented by the SSF through actuarial valuations for each of the schemes as a major planning tool for maintaining the long-term future financial sustainability of these schemes as well as the adequacy of the respective benefits and coverage. By providing a solid quantitative framework, actuarial valuations will guide future policy decisions with regard to the individual schemes. Only with the help of sound financial analysis can decision-makers, the social partners and the population at large decide democratically how to enhance and modernize their social security schemes. Reliable data, as a starting point of the valuations as well as requisites for setting future actuarial assumptions to control the dynamics of the scheme, is essential to carry out reliable actuarial valuations. Even though the projection methodologies techniques were the most sophisticated and robust, results of the valuation would be seriously undermined and become unreliable without reliable data. It is therefore important to engage not only the services of an actuary but also of a statistician in order to collect reliable data. The actuary will provide individual actuarial reports for each social security scheme presenting the methodology and results of each actuarial valuation. The principle purpose of the actuarial report is to show how the cost of a given scheme will evolve. However, it also has the objective of presenting a summary of the methods used so that another actuary will be able to understand the bases of the valuation, enabling the report to be reconciled to the next actuarial review. The actuarial report is also important in that it presents complementary information on benefit levels, administration, statistics and investments that are hard to find in other documents on social security schemes. The actuarial reports for each scheme should provide a clear picture of the financial situation and future direction of each scheme, allowing another actuary to be able to make an appraisal of the valuation

rows.reporting: International Labour Organization (ILO)
rows.title: Emergency employment response to Nepal earthquake-affected areas: Provision of OSH equipment for debris and landslide clearance workers
rows.description: To ensure that the ILO can rapidly respond to the on-going emergency and launch recovery operations in the immediate aftermath of the crisis, the office will need to strengthen its capacity from now until at least the end of July 2015 in the following areas: 1. Surge capacities at CO Kathmandu: Two specialists have already been deployed to provide technical support on post-disaster labour-intensive infrastructure rehabilitation activities and to support ILO’s participation in UNCT/HCT. 2. Rapid assessment of employment and livelihoods impact: Macroeconomic specialists from ROAP and DWTs in Bangkok and New Delhi are currently compiling figures on the estimated loss on employment and livelihoods. This estimate will be included in UN SitReps, disseminated to the media and also used in preparing the forthcoming PDNA and Flash Appeals. 3. Post-Disaster Needs Assessment (PDNA): A PDNA is expected to be launched in three weeks. BKK will assist CO Kathmandu in coordinating ILO participation with relevant HQ branches, such as DEVINVEST. Under this overall response framework, the contribution directly contributes to creating 1,500 emergency employment opportunities, as well as enhancing the safety of those workers by providing protection clothing and hand tools. The activities are envisaged in 10 districts including Sindulpalchowk, Gorkha, Lamjung, Tanahu, Dhading, Kaski, Palpa, Makwanpur, Nuwakot and Rasuwa.

rows.reporting: Mannion Danniels
rows.title: UK Aid Direct: A challenge fund designed to support the UK’s commitments to achieving the Global Goals.
rows.description:
Funded by the UK’s Department for International Development (DFID), UK Aid Direct was established in 2014 as a successor to the Global Poverty Action Fund (GPAF), which was created in 2010. UK Aid Direct is a challenge fund designed to support the UK’s commitments to achieving the Global Goals. The aim of UK Aid Direct is to fund small- and medium-sized national and international civil society organisations (CSOs) to reduce poverty and work towards achieving the Global Goals. Specifically, UK Aid Direct funding reaches the most marginalised and vulnerable populations, supporting the DFID agenda to ‘leave no one behind’. This agenda can be achieved through funding projects that encompass service delivery, economic empowerment, strengthening accountability or generating social change. As a flexible fund, UK Aid Direct is designed to be an adaptive and demand-led fund that responds to DFID priorities of:

- Strengthening global peace, security and governance

- Strengthening resilience and response to crisis

- Promoting global prosperity

- Tackling extreme poverty and helping the world’s most vulnerable

- Delivering value for money

rows.reporting: Mannion Danniels
rows.title: UK Aid Match II
rows.description: UK Aid Match II is the second phase of the UK Aid Match programme and is the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) fund for match funding UK public donations to charity appeals. It aims to provide opportunities for the UK public to have a say in how UK aid is spent by offering to match every £1 donated by the public to a UK Aid Match charity appeal, and charities use the match funding raised in the appeals to implement projects that improve the lives of some of the world’s poorest and most vulnerable people.

rows.reporting: Aqua for All
rows.title: Support for minority women toilet operators during COVID-19 lockdown in Nepal
rows.description: Aerosan operates low-cost public sanitation facilities in Nepal’s capital Kathmandu, connected to wastewater treatment. During the COVID-19 pandemic, a lockdown was imposed, leaving the streets of Kathmandu virtually empty with consequently no users at the sanitation facilities. The Response Facility has supported Aerosan’s operators during this lockdown and has helped 20 highly trained and experienced staff in both the Nepal office and at the public toilets. The fund provided salaries during the lockdown and supported the training of new Dalit female members of the Sanitation Workers Co-op. As a result, Aerosan was able to resume operations immediately once lockdown came to an end.
